text: Day. Under the law of grace, Christians sing this and such like canticles. (Worthington)<hr>Sion. This word is not in Hebrew, etc., though it be understood. (Calmet)<hr>Other nations have their respective cities. All Christians admit this one. (Worthington)<hr>The captives continue to return thanks. Yet the Holy Ghost speaks chiefly of the Church, and of the general resurrection. (Calmet)<hr>Bulwark. Faith and good works. (Worthington)
Truth. The Jews who returned from Babylon, were more virtuous than their ancestors, as the prophets intimate; though they have Christians principally in view.
Away: condemning the virtuous, as if they were fools. (Menochius)<hr>Symmachus, "our work, or fiction, is taken away." Hebrew may have other meanings. (Haydock)
Needy. The Jews shall behold the ruin of the city by Cyrus, (Calmet) who was of a contemptible nation. (Haydock)
In. God will remove every obstacle, at their return.
Justice. Clemency would therefore be ill placed. If the Israelites had not been led away captives, would they ever have been reformed?
Not see. Let them perish, or live to witness the glory of the Jews.
Works, both in punishing and rewarding. (Calmet)<hr>God crowns his own gifts. (Estius)
Lords of Babylon, (Calmet) and our own passions. (Haydock)
Giants; the proud emperors of Babylon, whom thou wilt destroy. Septuagint, "physicians;" as Rephaim has also this meaning.

Nation of the Jews. (Calmet)<hr>Septuagint, "add evils to them, O Lord; add evils to the nobles of the land." (Haydock)<hr>Hebrew may have the same sense.<hr>Ends: princes, or the Chaldeans, sending them also into captivity; or thou hast propagated thy Church over the world.
They. Septuagint, "We," etc. (Calmet)<hr>Affliction is a wholesome medicine. (Haydock)

Wind. Our expectation of aid from others has been disappointed. (Calmet)<hr>Septuagint, "the spirit of thy salvation, which thou hast wrought on the earth. We shall not fall, but the inhabitants of the earth shall fall." (Haydock)<hr>Their copies must have been different from ours.<hr>Fallen. The Chanaanites are left for our trial and punishment. (Calmet)
Dead: a civil death, shall regain their liberty; and those who have left this world in a state of virtue, shall be happy.<hr>Ruin. Cyrus liberated the Jews, having conquered Babylon.
Away, and Cambyses be destroyed, Ezechiel 38:11. (Calmet)
Shall cover her stain no more. This is said with relation to the martyrs, and their happy resurrection. (Challoner)<hr>The blood of the saints shall demand vengeance. (Calmet)
